devanagari: सबालवत्सवृन्दे ब्रह्मणापहृते श्रीकृष्णस्य । तत्तद्रूपेणाब्दं यथापूर्वं विहरणं ब्रह्मणो मोहभंगश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । साधु पृष्टं महाभाग त्...
english_devnagari: sabālavatsavṛnde brahmaṇāpahṛte śrīkṛṣṇasya tattadrūpeṇābdaṃ yathāpūrvaṃ viharaṇaṃ brahmaṇo mohabhaṃgaśca śrīśuka uvāca sādhu pṛṣṭaṃ mahābhāga tvayā bhāgavatottama yannūtanayasīśasya śrṛṇvannapi kathāṃ muhuḥ

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
A good query has been made by you, Oh highly fortunate king. By listening to the stories of the Lord again and again, you bring new charm to them (by your intelligent questions), Oh best of Hari’s votaries.

devanagari: सतामयं सारभृतां निसर्गो । यदर्थवाणी श्रुतिचेतसामपि । प्रतिक्षणं नव्यवदच्युतस्य यत् । स्त्रिया विटानामिव साधु वार्ता ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: satāmayaṃ sārabhṛtāṃ nisargo yadarthavāṇī śruticetasāmapi pratikṣaṇaṃ navyavadacyutasya yat striyā viṭānāmiva sādhu vārtā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: It is the nature of the righteous persons who assimilate only the essentials, that, just as the topic of young damsels is ever fresh and fascinating to gallants[1], the auspicious stories of Lord Acyuta (who never slips from rasa appear with fresh sweetness every moment to them, though their speech, ears and minds are devoted to them).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
If an extremely disgustible object like a woman can become an object of ever fresh sweetness and love, what need be said about the glorious Lord—VT.

devanagari: श्रृणुष्वावहितो राजन् अपि गुह्यं वदामि ते । ब्रूयुः स्निग्धस्य शिष्यस्य गुरवो गुह्यमप्युत ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrṛṇuṣvāvahito rājan api guhyaṃ vadāmi te brūyuḥ snigdhasya śiṣyasya guravo guhyamapyuta

translation: Listen to me attentively, Oh king, for I am disclosing to you a secret; for preceptors confide what is even confidential and most hidden, to their loving disciples.

devanagari: तथा अघवदनान्मृत्यो रक्षित्वा वत्सपालकान् । सरित्पुलिनमानीय भगवान् इदमब्रवीत् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā aghavadanānmṛtyo rakṣitvā vatsapālakān saritpulinamānīya bhagavān idamabravīt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

translation: Having saved the cowherd boys from death in the mouth of the demon Agha in the aforesaid manner, the Lord brought them to the sandy bank of the river, and spoke as follows:

devanagari: अहोऽतिरम्यं पुलिनं वयस्याः । स्वकेलिसम्पन् मृदुलाच्छबालुकम् । स्फुटत्सरोगन्ध हृतालिपत्रिक । ध्वनिप्रतिध्वानलसद् द्रुमाकुलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: aho svakelisampan mṛdulācchabālukam sphuṭatsarogandha hṛtālipatrika dhvanipratidhvānalasad drumākulam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sphuṭ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphuṭ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sphuṭ class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ las class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ las class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ las class 1 verb]

translation: “Extremely beautiful is this bank of soft, clean, stainless sand, so spacious and suitable for our sports, oh friends! It is encircled by beautiful trees, rendered fascinating by the echoing notes and humming of bees attracted here by the sweet smell of blooming lotuses.

devanagari: अत्र भोक्तव्यमस्माभिः दिवारूढं क्षुधार्दिताः । वत्साः समीपेऽपः पीत्वा चरन्तु शनकैस्तृणम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: atra bhoktavyamasmābhiḥ divārūḍhaṃ kṣudhārditāḥ vatsāḥ samīpe
translation: Let us have our meal here. It is high time now as the day is considerably advanced and we are oppressed with hunger. Let the calves drink water and leisurely graze the grass nearby.”

devanagari: तथेति पाययित्वार्भा वत्सानारुध्य शाद्वले । मुक्त्वा शिक्यानि बुभुजुः समं भगवता मुदा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatheti pāyayitvārbhā vatsānārudhya śādvale muktvā śikyāni bubhujuḥ samaṃ bhagavatā mudā
translation: Approving of his proposal, (lit. saying ‘so be it’), they led the calves to drink water and set them to graze in the green pasture. Untying their slings (and opening their tiffinboxes), they enjoyed the lunch with the Lord in great delight.

devanagari: कृष्णस्य विष्वक् पुरुराजिमण्डलैः । अभ्याननाः फुल्लदृशो व्रजार्भकाः । सहोपविष्टा विपिने विरेजुः । छदा यथाम्भोरुहकर्णिकायाः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasya viṣvak pururājimaṇḍalaiḥ abhyānanāḥ phulladṛśo vrajārbhakāḥ sahopaviṣṭā vipine virejuḥ chadā yathāmbhoruhakarṇikāyāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ phull class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ phull class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ phull class 1 verb]

translation: Sitting together closely round Kṛṣṇa in a number of circular rows, and all of them facing him[2] with their eyes blooming fully, the boys of Vraja shone (looked brilliant) like lotus-petals grown round the pericarp (of that lotus).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
VC. explains that it was Kṛṣṇa who, being ‘possessed of hands, feet, eyes, heads and faces in all directions’ (sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādaṃ tat sarvato'kṣi- śiro-mukham) faced each of them, making each elated to feel that he alone had the privilege of facing him directly—a blessing denied toothers.
KD.: The boys lost their individuality in Kṛṣṇa (13.24-28).

devanagari: केचित् पुष्पैर्दलैः केचित् पल्लवैः अङ्कुरैः फलैः । शिग्भिः त्वग्भिः दृषद्भिश्च बुभुजुः कृतभाजनाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: kecit puṣpairdalaiḥ kecit pallavaiḥ aṅkuraiḥ phalaiḥ śigbhiḥ tvagbhiḥ dṛṣadbhiśca bubhujuḥ kṛtabhājanāḥ
translation: Some improvised flowers and leaves as dining plates, some tender leaves and shoots, some fruits, some their walletslings, some barks and slabs of stones (for plates), and they enjoyed their meals.

devanagari: सर्वे मिथो दर्शयन्तः स्वस्वभोज्यरुचिं पृथक् । हसन्तो हासयन्तश्च अभ्यवजह्रुः सहेश्वराः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sarve mitho darśayantaḥ svasvabhojyaruciṃ pṛthak hasanto hāsayantaśca abhyavajahruḥ saheśvarāḥ

translation: (Even though each boy participated something of the dishes, others had brought) each boy severally showed (by gestures etc.) the special taste of his own dish, laughing himself and making all others laugh. (In this way) all took their meals with the Lord (who also tasted something of the dish brought by each of them).

devanagari: बिभ्रद् वेणुं जठरपटयोः श्रृङ्गवेत्रे च कक्षे । वामे पाणौ मसृणकवलं तत्फलान्यङ्गुलीषु । तिष्ठन् मध्ये स्वपरिसुहृदो हासयन् नर्मभिः स्वैः ...
english_devnagari: bibhrad veṇuṃ jaṭharapaṭayoḥ śrṛṅgavetre ca kakṣe vāme pāṇau masṛṇakavalaṃ tatphalānyaṅgulīṣu tiṣṭhan madhye svaparisuhṛdo hāsayan narmabhiḥ svaiḥ svarge loke miṣati bubhuje yajñabhug bālakeliḥ

translation: The presiding Deity and the Enjoyer of sacrifices as he was, he indulged in child-like sports, and with his flute between his belly and his pitāmbara (silken loin cloth[3]) and his horn and cane under the left arm (pit), and holding a soft morsel of boiled rice mixed with curds and (choice eatable) fruits held in between his fingers of his left hand, he occupied a central position in the circle of his friends, and made them to laugh by cracking jokes—And gods from the celestial regions simply looked on (envious that they are not lucky enough to participate).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Belt-like garment round the waist. (KD).

devanagari: भारतैवं वत्सपेषु भुञ्जानेष्वच्युतात्मसु । वत्सास्त्वन्तर्वने दूरं विविशुस्तृणलोभिताः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhārataivaṃ vatsapeṣu bhuñjāneṣvacyutātmasu vatsāstvantarvane dūraṃ viviśustṛṇalobhitāḥ

[nominative plural from √ lubh ], [vocative plural from √ lubh ], [accusative plural from √ lubh ]

translation: As the cowherd boys whose minds were absorbed in Lord Kṛṣṇa were having their repast, the calves, being allured by luxuriantly grown pasture, strayed away deep into the woods, oh scion of Bharata.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe trayodaśo
translation: Perceiving that they were overwhelmed with fear (at the straying away of calves), Kṛṣṇa, the terror to the terrors of the world, reassured them, saying, “Friends, (Don’t worry—lit. cast aside all your fears). Please do not disturb your meal. I shall fetch all the calves here”.

devanagari: तान् दृष्ट्वा भयसंत्रस्न् ऊचे कृष्णोऽस्य भीभयम् । मित्राण्याशान्मा विरमत् इहानेष्ये वत्सकानहम् । इत्युक्त्वाद्रिदरीकुञ्ज गह्वरेष्वात्म...
english_devnagari: tān dṛṣṭvā bhayasaṃtrasn ūce kṛṣṇo mitrāṇyāśānmā viramat ihāneṣye vatsakānaham ityuktvādridarīkuñja gahvareṣvātmavatsakān vicinvan bhagavān kṛṣṇaḥ sapāṇikavalo yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: With these words, while the morsel of boiled rice mixed with curds was in his hand, Kṛṣṇa went in search of his calves over the mountains, in caverns, in bushes and thick jungle.

devanagari: अम्भोजन्मजनिस्तदन्तरगतो मायार्भकस्येशितुः । द्रष्टुं मञ्जु महित्वमन्यदपि तद्वत्सानितो वत्सपान् । नीत्वान्यत्र कुरूद्वहान्तरदधात् खेऽवस...
english_devnagari: ambhojanmajanistadantaragato māyārbhakasyeśituḥ draṣṭuṃ mañju mahitvamanyadapi tadvatsānito vatsapān nītvānyatra kurūdvahāntaradadhāt khe dṛṣṭvāghāsuramokṣaṇaṃ prabhavataḥ prāptaḥ paraṃ vismayam

[nominative single from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tṝ class 1 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: God Brahmā (the lotus-born god)[4] who formerly stood in heaven to witness the release (of the cowherd boys and calves) from Aghāsura and was greatly astonished, desired to see another sweet feat of the Lord who assumed the form of a child through his Māyā power, and he lifted up the calves and abducted the cowherd boys, and carried them to some place and vanished himself.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
VC. suggests a pun on jala (jaḍa) due to the interchangeability of -d- and -l-. A lotus is born out of Jala (water) which is jaḍa (wanting in intelligence) and god Brahmā is born out of that knowledgeless inert being called lotus. Hence his own intelligence shares the stupidity of its heredity.

devanagari: ततो वत्सान् अदृष्ट्वैत्य पुलिनेऽपि च वत्सपान् । उभौ अपि वने कृष्णो विचिकाय समन्ततः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato vatsān adṛṣṭvaitya puline ubhau api vane kṛṣṇo vicikāya samantataḥ
translation: Not being able to find the calves, and failing to see the cowherd boys on the sandy bank, Kṛṣṇa searched for both of them all round.

devanagari: क्वाप्यदृष्ट्वान्तर्विपिने वत्सान् पालांश्च विश्ववित् । सर्वं विधिकृतं कृष्णः सहसावजगाम ह ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kvāpyadṛṣṭvāntarvipine vatsān pālāṃśca viśvavit sarvaṃ vidhikṛtaṃ kṛṣṇaḥ sahasāvajagāma ha
translation: Not finding either the calves or their protectors (cowherd boys) anywhere within that forest, Kṛṣṇa who knows everything in the Universe, understood it to be the work of god Brahmā.

devanagari: ततः कृष्णो मुदं कर्तुं तन्मातॄणां च कस्य च । उभयायितमात्मानं चक्रे विश्वकृदीश्वरः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kṛṣṇo mudaṃ kartuṃ tanmātṝṇāṃ ca kasya ca ubhayāyitamātmānaṃ cakre viśvakṛdīśvaraḥ

translation: Thereupon, with a view to bring joy to the mothers of the cowherd-boys (by restoring their children and calves), as well as to god Brahmā (by allowing the boys and calves to remain in his charge), Kṛṣṇa, the Maker and the Controller of the Universe, converted himself both (into the cowherd boys and the calves).

devanagari: यावद् वत्सपवत्सकाल्पकवपुः । र्यावत् कराङ्घ्र्यादिकं । यावद् यष्टिविषाणवेणुदलशिग् । यावद् विभूषाम्बरम् । यावत् शीलगुणाभिधाकृतिवयो । याव...
english_devnagari: yāvad vatsapavatsakālpakavapuḥ ryāvat karāṅghryādikaṃ yāvad yaṣṭiviṣāṇaveṇudalaśig yāvad vibhūṣāmbaram yāvat śīlaguṇābhidhākṛtivayo yāvad vihārādikaṃ sarvaṃ viṣṇumayaṃ giro sarvasvarūpo babhau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having exactly changed him into the same size and number of small bodies of the cowherd boys and calves, with same proportion and size of their limbs like hand, feet etc., taking with them the same (number and size of) sticks, horns, flutes, decorative leaves on the head, slings and wearing the same clothes and ornaments, and assuming the same goodness of nature (or character), accomplishments, name, form and age as well as their ways of sporting etc. (of each individual), the birthless Lord Kṛṣṇa, being thus identical with all forms, shone there as a concrete illustration, proving the veracity of the Vedic text: “Everything in the universe is pervaded by Viṣṇu.”

devanagari: स्वयमात्मात्मगोवत्सान प्रतिवार्यात्मवत्सपैः । क्रीडन् आत्मविहारैश्च सर्वात्मा प्राविशद् व्रजम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: svayamātmātmagovatsāna prativāryātmavatsapaiḥ krīḍan ātmavihāraiśca sarvātmā prāviśad vrajam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mav class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mav class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ mav class 1 verb]

translation: Having converted himself into the form of calves which were tended back by his ownself in the form of cowherd boys, and playing with his own forms, Kṛṣṇa, the self and the self of all, entered Vraja.

devanagari: तत्तद् वत्सान् पृथङ्नीत्वा तत्तद्‍गोष्ठे निवेश्य सः । तत्तद् आत्माभवद् राजन् तत्तत्सद्म प्रविष्टवान् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tattad vatsān pṛthaṅnītvā tattad‍goṣṭhe niveśya saḥ tattad ātmābhavad rājan tattatsadma praviṣṭavān

translation: Having taken the respective calves separately to their own houses, and tying them into their respective stalls, he entered the respective houses of the cowherd boys and stayed there in their form, oh King.

devanagari: तन्मातरो वेणुरवत्वरोत्थिता । उत्थाप्य दोर्भिः परिरभ्य निर्भरम् । स्नेहस्नुतस्तन्यपयःसुधासवं । मत्वा परं ब्रह्म सुतानपाययन् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tanmātaro veṇuravatvarotthitā utthāpya dorbhiḥ parirabhya nirbharam snehasnutastanyapayaḥsudhāsavaṃ matvā paraṃ brahma sutānapāyayan

translation: Rising hurriedly ar the sound of the flutes, the mothers (of the cowherd boys) raised them up and hugged them fast in their arms. With their breasts overflowing with milk of motherly affection which was sweet like nectar and inebriating, they suckled the Para Brahman mistaking him for their children.

devanagari: ततो नृपोन्मर्दनमज्जलेपन । अलङ्कार रक्षा तिलकाशनादिभिः । संलालितः स्वाचरितैः प्रहर्षयन् । सायं गतो यामयमेन माधवः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato nṛponmardanamajjalepana alaṅkāra rakṣā tilakāśanādibhiḥ saṃlālitaḥ svācaritaiḥ praharṣayan sāyaṃ gato yāmayamena mādhavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ sā class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ sā class 4 verb]

translation: Observing the time-schedule of games etc., during different parts of the day, and returning in time in the evening at the usual time, Kṛṣṇa (the consort of Lakṣmī) in the form of a cowherd boy) was fondled and treated with shampooing, bathing, anointing (with sandal paste, pigments etc.), decorating (with ornaments), evil-warding rites, applying the decorative mark (tilaka) on the forehead, feeding (with dishes) etc. (by the Gopīs). He also filled them with great joy by his sportive winsome ways.

devanagari: गावस्ततो गोष्ठमुपेत्य सत्वरं । हुङ्कारघोषैः परिहूतसङ्गतान् । स्वकान् स्वकान् वत्सतरानपाययन् । मुहुर्लिहन्त्यः स्रवदौधसं पयः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: gāvastato goṣṭhamupetya satvaraṃ huṅkāraghoṣaiḥ parihūtasaṅgatān svakān svakān vatsatarānapāyayan muhurlihantyaḥ sravadaudhasaṃ payaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ lih class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ lih class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ lih class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ lih class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ lih class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ lih class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ sru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sru class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sru class 1 verb]

translation: Thereupon the cows returned hurriedly to their own pens, and inviting their respective calves by their specific lowing-calls, suckled them with milk overflowing from their udders, licking the calves (all over), every now and then.

devanagari: गोगोपीनां मातृतास्मिन् सर्वा स्नेहर्धिकां विना । पुरोवदास्वपि हरेः तोकता मायया विना ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: gogopīnāṃ mātṛtāsmin sarvā snehardhikāṃ vinā purovadāsvapi hareḥ tokatā māyayā vinā
translation: The maternal affection of the kine and the Gopīs towards him (in the form of calves and cowherd boys), was the same as before, except that their affection grew more ardent, and Hari’s behaviour towards them as (their) child was the same as before, but there was no delusion (in Kṛṣṇa’s relation with them, as was in the case of their real children).

devanagari: व्रजौकसां स्वतोकेषु स्नेहवल्ल्याब्दमन्वहम् । शनैर्निःसीम ववृधे यथा कृष्णे त्वपूर्ववत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: vrajaukasāṃ svatokeṣu snehavallyābdamanvaham śanairniḥsīma vavṛdhe yathā kṛṣṇe tvapūrvavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: (In this way) for one year, the affection of the residents of Vraja towards their children grew gradually every day (as they were the parts aṃśas of the Lord), but in the case of Kṛṣṇa it developed in an unprecedent way and became unlimited (as he was the aṃśin—the Lord himself).

devanagari: इत्थं आत्माऽत्मनाऽत्मानं वत्सपालमिषेण सः । पालयन् वत्सपो वर्षं चिक्रीडे वनगोष्ठयोः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ ātmā pālayan vatsapo varṣaṃ cikrīḍe vanagoṣṭhayoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pāl class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ pāl class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ pā ], [vocative single from √ pā ]

translation: In this way, protecting and being tended himself in the form of calves, by himself, in the capacity of cowherd boys, he sported himself for one year, in the forest, as well as in Vraja.

devanagari: एकदा चारयन् वत्सान् सरामो वनमाविशत् । पञ्चषासु त्रियामासु हायनापूरणीष्वजः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā cārayan vatsān sarāmo vanamāviśat pañcaṣāsu triyāmāsu hāyanāpūraṇīṣvajaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ car ], [vocative single from √ car ]

translation: One day, while it was some five or six days to complete the period of a year, Kṛṣṇa, along with Balarāma entered the forest, while they were tending the calves.

devanagari: ततो विदूराच्चरतो गावो वत्सानुपव्रजम् । गोवर्धनाद्रिशिरसि चरन्त्यो ददृशुस्तृणम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tato vidūrāccarato gāvo vatsānupavrajam govardhanādriśirasi carantyo dadṛśustṛṇam
translation: The cows which were grazing grass on the top of the Govardhana hill saw from afar their calves pasturing near Vraja.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वाथ तत्स्नेहवशोऽस्मृतात्मा । स गोव्रजोऽत्यात्मप दुर्गमार्गः । द्विपात्ककुद्‍ग्रीव उदास्यपुच्छो । अगाद्धुङ्कृतैरास्रुपया जवेन ॥ ३...
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvātha tatsnehavaśo sa govrajo dvipātkakud‍grīva udāsyapuccho agāddhuṅkṛtairāsrupayā javena

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: As soon as the herd of cows saw them, they being overpowered with affection for the calves, forgot themselves. They rushed by a path difficult to be followed by their protectors (cowherds). Like bipeds (due to fast running on all fours), with their necks contracted towards their humps and with tails raised up, they dashed on speedily bellowing (all the while), and with milk oozing out of their udders, all the way.

devanagari: समेत्य गावोऽधो वत्सान् वत्सवत्योऽप्यपाययन् । गिलन्त्य इव चाङ्गानि लिहन्त्यः स्वौधसं पयः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: sametya gāvo gilantya iva cāṅgāni lihantyaḥ svaudhasaṃ payaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ lih class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ lih class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ lih class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ lih class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ lih class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ lih class 2 verb]

translation: Coming down to the foot of the Govardhana hill, the cows, though they had their own calves, suckled them with the milk from their udders, licking the bodies of the calves (with such intense eagerness) as if they wanted to swallow them.

devanagari: गोपाः तद् रोधनायास मौघ्यलज्जोरुमन्युना । दुर्गाध्वकृच्छ्रतोऽभ्येत्य गोवत्सैर्ददृशुः सुतान् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: gopāḥ tad rodhanāyāsa maughyalajjorumanyunā durgādhvakṛcchrato
translation: Being extremely angry at their disgrace and inability in restraining and controlling the cows, and making their way with great difficulty through the inaccessible hilly track, they saw their sons, along with the calves.

devanagari: तदीक्षणोत्प्रेमरसाप्लुताशया । जातानुरागा गतमन्यवोऽर्भकान् । उदुह्य दोर्भिः परिरभ्य मूर्धनि । घ्राणैरवापुः परमां मुदं ते ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tadīkṣaṇotpremarasāplutāśayā jātānurāgā gatamanyavo uduhya dorbhiḥ parirabhya mūrdhani ghrāṇairavāpuḥ paramāṃ mudaṃ te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [aorist active second single]
[imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]

translation: With their hearts submerged in (i.e. overwhelmed with) affection at the sight of their children and with filial love surging in them, they forgot their anger, and raising their children with their arms and embracing them, they smelt on the crowns of their heads and were enraptured.

devanagari: ततः प्रवयसो गोपाः तोकाश्लेषसुनिर्वृताः । कृच्छ्रात् शनैरपगताः तदनुस्मृत्युदश्रवः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ pravayaso gopāḥ tokāśleṣasunirvṛtāḥ kṛcchrāt śanairapagatāḥ tadanusmṛtyudaśravaḥ
translation: Then the grown-up Gopas who felt extremely happy at the embrace of their children, withdrew slowly with a heavy heart from that place with tears gushing out at their memory.

devanagari: व्रजस्य रामः प्रेमर्धेः वीक्ष्यौत्कण्ठ्यमनुक्षणम् । मुक्तस्तनेष्वपत्येषु अहेतुविद् अचिन्तयत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: vrajasya rāmaḥ premardheḥ vīkṣyautkaṇṭhyamanukṣaṇam muktastaneṣvapatyeṣu ahetuvid acintayat
translation: Observing the intense longing and love growing every moment in the heart of Vraja (people and the cows) for their children (and calves), though weaned away from their mother’s breast, Balarāma failed to understand its reason and began to think:

devanagari: किमेतद् अद्‍भुतमिव वासुदेवेऽखिलात्मनि । व्रजस्य सात्मनस्तोकेषु अपूर्वं प्रेम वर्धते ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kimetad ad‍bhutamiva vāsudeve vrajasya sātmanastokeṣu apūrvaṃ prema vardhate
translation: “It is really wonderful that the affection of the people of Vraja—and I am no exception to it—towards their children is increasing in intensity by leaps and bounds as never before, as it did in the case of Vāsudeva, the soul of the whole of the universe.

devanagari: केयं वा कुत आयाता दैवी वा नार्युतासुरी । प्रायो मायास्तु मे भर्तुः नान्या मेऽपि विमोहिनी ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: keyaṃ vā kuta āyātā daivī vā nāryutāsurī prāyo māyāstu me bhartuḥ nānyā me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ki class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ ki class 3 verb]

translation: What infatuation is this? Whence has it emerged? Is this delusion caused by gods, men or demons? Most probably it must be the deluding potency of my Lord and of none else, as it enchants even me (No other Māyā has this power of deluding me)”.

devanagari: इति सञ्चिन्त्य दाशार्हो वत्सान् सवयसानपि । सर्वानाचष्ट वैकुण्ठं चक्षुषा वयुनेन सः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sañcintya dāśārho vatsān savayasānapi sarvānācaṣṭa vaikuṇṭhaṃ cakṣuṣā vayunena saḥ
translation: Giving deep thought to the matter, Balarāma, of the Dāśārha clan, saw through his spiritual eye (lit. the eye of knowledge) that all the calves and his cowherd friends were the Lord Kṛṣṇa himself.

devanagari: नैते सुरेशा ऋषयो न चैते । त्वमेव भासीश भिदाश्रयेऽपि । सर्वं पृथक्त्वं निगमात् कथं वदे । त्युक्तेन वृत्तं प्रभुणा बलोऽवैत् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: naite sureśā ṛṣayo na caite tvameva bhāsīśa bhidāśraye sarvaṃ pṛthaktvaṃ nigamāt kathaṃ vade tyuktena vṛttaṃ prabhuṇā balo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: [5] (Balarāma asked Kṛṣṇa)—“These (cowherd boys) are not the rulers of gods. Nor are these (cows) the sages (as I know them to be). It is you who appear in these different forms. Be pleased to tell me, how all this took place.” The Lord briefly explained which Balarāma could grasp correctly.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
(1) SS. Balarama wanted to know how the Lord though one became many. Here SS. quotes Tait-Up. 2.5 vijñānaṃ cāvijñānaṃ ca satyaṃ cānṛtaṃ ca satyam abhavat.
(2) Bhāgavata Candrikā. ‘Be pleased to tell how you who are the one, came to be many.’ Kṛṣṇa explained that as stated in the Vedas, He accomplished it through His Will. Balarāma came to understand it now.

devanagari: तावदेत्यात्मभूरात्ममानेन त्रुट्यनेहसा । पुरोवदाब्दं क्रीडन्तं ददृशे सकलं हरिम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tāvadetyātmabhūrātmamānena truṭyanehasā purovadābdaṃ krīḍantaṃ dadṛśe sakalaṃ harim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ truṭ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ truṭ class 4 verb]

translation: When the self-born god (Brahmā) returned in a quarter of a moment or half (the time of winking of eyes) according to his measure of time which amounted to one human year, he saw Hari playing as before with all his companions, even at the end of the year.

devanagari: यावन्तो गोकुले बालाः सवत्साः सर्व एव हि । मायाशये शयाना मे नाद्यापि पुनरुत्थिताः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvanto gokule bālāḥ savatsāḥ sarva eva hi māyāśaye śayānā me nādyāpi punarutthitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śī class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ śī class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nad ]
[vocative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nad ]
[nominative single from √ nad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ nad ]

translation: “Whatever number of children there was in Gokula along with the calves—all of them are stupefied and lying in the bed of my Māyā-power and none of them has awakened.

devanagari: इत एतेऽत्र कुत्रत्या मन्माया मोहितेतरे । तावन्त एव तत्राब्दं क्रीडन्तो विष्णुना समम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: ita ete tāvanta eva tatrābdaṃ krīḍanto viṣṇunā samam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ muh ], [locative single from √ muh ]
[nominative dual from √ muh ], [vocative single from √ muh ], [vocative dual from √ muh ], [accusative dual from √ muh ], [locative single from √ muh ]
[nominative single from √ muh ], [nominative dual from √ muh ], [vocative single from √ muh ], [vocative dual from √ muh ], [accusative dual from √ muh ]
[nominative plural from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]

translation: Who are these and whence have they come as they are other than those who are under the spell of my deluding power? They are the same in number and have been playing with Kṛṣṇa (Viṣṇu—the all-pervading Lord) for a period of one year.

devanagari: एवमेतेषु भेदेषु चिरं ध्यात्वा स आत्मभूः । सत्याः के कतरे नेति ज्ञातुं नेष्टे कथञ्चन ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: evameteṣu bhedeṣu ciraṃ dhyātvā sa ātmabhūḥ satyāḥ ke katare neti jñātuṃ neṣṭe kathañcana

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ neṣ class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb]

translation: Pondering for a long time on these differences (between children under his spell and those playing with Kṛṣṇa), the self-born god Brahmā could not, even with difficulty, distinguish between those (children and calves) who were real and who were not.

devanagari: एवं सम्मोहयन् विष्णुं विमोहं विश्वमोहनम् । स्वयैव माययाजोऽपि स्वयमेव विमोहितः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sammohayan viṣṇuṃ vimohaṃ viśvamohanam svayaiva māyayājo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Thus in his attempt to delude Viṣṇu (Kṛṣṇa) who transcends all delusion, yet is deluding the whole universe, god Brahmā found himself enchanted by his own deluding capacity.

devanagari: तम्यां तमोवन्नैहारं खद्योतार्चिरिवाहनि । महतीतरमायैश्यं निहन्त्यात्मनि युञ्जतः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tamyāṃ tamovannaihāraṃ khadyotārcirivāhani mahatītaramāyaiśyaṃ nihantyātmani yuñjataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [ablative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [genitive single from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[ablative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [genitive single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: Just as the darkness caused by a drop of mist (becomes absorbed in the darkness of night, and like the light emitted by the fire-fly gets merged in the broad day-light, similarly lower type of māyā (deluding power) when used against a superior person loses its effectiveness and is lost to the user.

devanagari: तावत्सर्वे वत्सपालाः पश्यतोऽजस्य तत्क्षणात् । व्यदृश्यन्त घनश्यामाः पीतकौशेयवाससः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvatsarve vatsapālāḥ paśyato vyadṛśyanta ghanaśyāmāḥ pītakauśeyavāsasaḥ
translation: And Lo! While god Brahmā was looking on, in a moment all the cowherds appeared dark as cloud in complexion and wearing silken yellow garments.

devanagari: चतुर्भुजाः शङ्खचक्र गदाराजीवपाणयः । किरीटिनः कुण्डलिनो हारिणो वनमालिनः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: caturbhujāḥ śaṅkhacakra gadārājīvapāṇayaḥ kirīṭinaḥ kuṇḍalino hāriṇo vanamālinaḥ
translation: (They were) endowed with four arms wielding in each hand a conch, a discus, a mace and a lotus; (they were) wearing crowns, ear-rings, pearl-necklace and wreaths of sylvain flowers.

devanagari: श्रीवत्साङ्गददोरत्‍न कम्बुकङ्कणपाणयः । नूपुरैः कटकैर्भाताः कटिसूत्राङ्गुलीयकैः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvatsāṅgadadorat‍na kambukaṅkaṇapāṇayaḥ nūpuraiḥ kaṭakairbhātāḥ kaṭisūtrāṅgulīyakaiḥ
translation:
(They were) adorned with Śrī-vatsa (a curl of golden hair on the right bosom), armlets (aṅgada) and a pair of bejeweled bracelets marked with three lines like conchs, on their wrists.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā’s text:]
48(A). With their chest beautified by Śrī-vatsa and with kaustubha gem-pendant round the neck, they appeared shining with anklets, bangles, girdles and rings.

devanagari: आङ्घ्रिमस्तकमापूर्णाः तुलसीनवदामभिः । कोमलैः सर्वगात्रेषु भूरिपुण्यवदर्पितैः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: āṅghrimastakamāpūrṇāḥ tulasīnavadāmabhiḥ komalaiḥ sarvagātreṣu bhūripuṇyavadarpitaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nu class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nu class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ nu class 1 verb]

translation: All over their persons, from head to foot, they were beautified with wreaths of tender, fresh Tulasi offered to them by highly meritorious (devotees).

devanagari: चन्द्रिकाविशदस्मेरैः सारुणापाङ्गवीक्षितैः । स्वकार्थानामिव रजः सत्त्वाभ्यां स्रष्टृपालकाः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: candrikāviśadasmeraiḥ sāruṇāpāṅgavīkṣitaiḥ svakārthānāmiva rajaḥ sattvābhyāṃ sraṣṭṛpālakāḥ
translation: With their smiles bright like the moon-light (the whiteness of which represents the sattva-guṇa, whose function is the protection and preservation of the world) and with their side-long glances from their reddish eyes (the reddishness of which stands for the rajo-guṇa the function of which is the creation of the world), they looked like the creators and protectors of the desires of their votaries (they fulfilled whatever desire aroused in the heart of their devotees) with the help of sattva and rajas.[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
They appeared like Protectors with their white sattva-like smiles and shone wiṃ their reddish side-glances like creators by the reddish- rajas guṇa—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: आत्मादिस्तम्बपर्यन्तैः मूर्तिमद्भिः चराचरैः । नृत्यगीताद्यनेकार्हैः पृथक् पृथक् उपासिताः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmādistambaparyantaiḥ mūrtimadbhiḥ carācaraiḥ nṛtyagītādyanekārhaiḥ pṛthak pṛthak upāsitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: They were adored severally by means of dancing, singing and offering many other courses of worship by all embodied beings, mobile and immobile, from Brahmā’s own self to a clump of grass, all endowed with a corporeal form.

devanagari: अणिमाद्यैर्महिमभिः अजाद्याभिर्विभूतिभिः । चतुर्विंशतिभिस्तत्त्वैः परीता महदादिभिः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: aṇimādyairmahimabhiḥ ajādyābhirvibhūtibhiḥ caturviṃśatibhistattvaiḥ parītā mahadādibhiḥ
translation: They were waited upon all around by mystic powers like aṇimā (power of becoming atomic in size) and by potencies such as Māyā, Vidyā and by twenty-four principles (enumerated by Sāṅkhyas) beginning with Mahat (—all assuming the forms of persons).

devanagari: कालस्वभावसंस्कार कामकर्मगुणादिभिः । स्वमहिध्वस्तमहिभिः मूर्तिमद्भिः उपासिताः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: kālasvabhāvasaṃskāra kāmakarmaguṇādibhiḥ svamahidhvastamahibhiḥ mūrtimadbhiḥ upāsitāḥ
translation: They were also attended upon by the Time-spirit[7] (which sets in motion the process of creation by disturbing the balance of the guṇas of Prakṛti), Nature, Saṃskāras (latent desires or proclivities), Desire (for enjoyment), Activity, the three guṇas—all assuming corporal forms, with their glories eclipsed by the Lord.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
C. Kālaḥ svabhāvo nīyatir yaddṛcchā etc.—ŚV. Up. 1.2

devanagari: सत्यज्ञानानन्तानन्द मात्रैकरसमूर्तयः । अस्पृष्टभूरिमाहात्म्या अपि ह्युपनिषद्दृशाम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: satyajñānānantānanda mātraikarasamūrtayaḥ aspṛṣṭabhūrimāhātmyā api hyupaniṣaddṛśām
translation: All of them were embodiments of the one absolute essence consisting of reality, knowledge and infinite bliss. Their greatness and glory were incomprehensible even to those who have obtained the vision of Upaniṣadic knowledge.

devanagari: एवं सकृद् ददर्शाजः परब्रह्मात्मनोऽखिलान् । यस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति सचराचरम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sakṛd dadarśājaḥ parabrahmātmano yasya bhāsā sarvamidaṃ vibhāti sacarācaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: It was only once that Brahmā espied that all those forms were identical with the Supreme Brahman by whose light the universe both mobile and immobile, animate and inanimate stands revealed.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
tasya bhāsā sarvam idaṃ vibhāti—Muṇḍ. Up. 2.2.10, Śv. Up. 6.14.

devanagari: ततोऽतिकुतुकोद्वृत्त स्तिमितैकादशेन्द्रियः । तद्धाम्नाभूदजस्तूष्णीं पूर्देव्यन्तीव पुत्रिका ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato taddhāmnābhūdajastūṣṇīṃ pūrdevyantīva putrikā
translation: Thereupon, when god Brahmā, out of great curiosity and wonder, directed his eyes elsewhere (towards himself or to his seat on the swan, he was so over-whelmed by the spiritual effulgence of the Lord that all his eleven sense-organs (five conative, five cognitive senses and the mind) became dazed and motionless; And he stood still and silent like a statuette of a secondary deity by the side of the presiding deity of that settlement (Vraja).

devanagari: इतीरेशेऽतर्क्ये निजमहिमनि स्वप्रमितिके । परत्राजातोऽतन् निरसनमुखब्रह्मकमितौ । अनीशेऽपि द्रष्टुं किमिदमिति वा मुह्यति सति । चछादाजो ज्ञ...
english_devnagari: itīreśe paratrājāto anīśe cachādājo jñātvā sapadi paramo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: When Brahmā, the Lord of Sarasvatī (the goddess of learning) who was incapable of comprehending Lord Kṛṣṇa’s (or the Supreme Brahman’s) special glory and greatness, became confused about Brahman which is beyond the reach of logical reasoning, and is thus incomprehensible—Brahman which stands resplendent in its perfect bliss; is far above and beyond Māyā, and is unborn, and can be known only through the Vedāntic (Upaniṣadic) texts which describe It in the negative terms (neti, neti—‘It is not so, It is not such’), and he could not even perceive the manifestation at all, Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Unborn Lord immediately understood god Brahmā’s perplexity, and drew off the curtain of his Māyā.

devanagari: ततोऽर्वाक्प्रतिलब्धाक्षः कः परेतवदुत्थितः । कृच्छ्राद् उन्मील्य वै दृष्टीः आचष्टेदं सहात्मना ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: tato kṛcchrād unmīlya vai dṛṣṭīḥ ācaṣṭedaṃ sahātmanā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Thereupon god Brahmā whose vision to view the outside world was restored, opened his eyes with difficulties like a person brought back to life from death, and verily saw again this whole universe along with himself.

devanagari: सपद्येवाभितः पश्यन् दिशोऽपश्यत् पुरःस्थितम् । वृन्दावनं जनाजीव्य द्रुमाकीर्णं समाप्रियम् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: sapadyevābhitaḥ paśyan diśo vṛndāvanaṃ janājīvya drumākīrṇaṃ samāpriyam
translation: Immediately he cast his eyes around in all directions and found stretched before him the (vista of) Vṛndāvana forest abounding in trees providing the means of subsistence to people, and with desirable objects[9] (necessities of life) in plenty.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
SS., VT. interpret ‘samapriya’ as:
(i) Vṛndāvana, Govardhana etc. Liked by Lord Kṛṣṇa or (ii) along with Lord Kṛṣṇa who was dear to goddess Lakṣmī (mā).

devanagari: यत्र नैसर्गदुर्वैराः सहासन् नृमृगादयः । मित्राणीवाजितावास द्रुतरुट्तर्षकादिकम् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: yatra naisargadurvairāḥ sahāsan nṛmṛgādayaḥ mitrāṇīvājitāvāsa drutaruṭtarṣakādikam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
In that forest, men, deer and other animals who are (normally) inimical by nature, lived together as friends, and from which hatred, covetiouness (and other passions) had disappeared due to the presence of Kṛṣṇa.
[Bhāvāratha Dīpikā) brings out the full significance of every attribute of Kṛṣṇa thus:]

devanagari: तत्रोद्वहत्पशुपवंशशिशुत्वनाट्यं । ब्रह्माद्वयं परमनन्तमगाधबोधम् । वत्सान् सखीनिव पुरा परितो विचिन्वद् । एकं सपाणिकवलं परमेष्ठ्यचष्ट ॥ ...
english_devnagari: tatrodvahatpaśupavaṃśaśiśutvanāṭyaṃ brahmādvayaṃ paramanantamagādhabodham vatsān sakhīniva purā parito vicinvad ekaṃ sapāṇikavalaṃ parameṣṭhyacaṣṭa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ naṭ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ naṭ ]
[nominative single from √ naṭ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ naṭ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ naṭ ], [accusative single from √ naṭ ]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: And Brahmā, the highest deity in the Universe, perceived the Lord acting before him the part of a child born in the family of cowherd—The Lord who, though without a second, was searching for calves, though absolutely one, was (trying to find) his friends (cowherd boys), though endowed with unfathomable knowledge, was attempting to search them (as if he did not know); though he was Infinite and omnipresent, he was looking around; though he was the Supreme Lord, he was behaving like a child, and though the Brahman himself, he held a morsel of food in his hand.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा त्वरेण निजधोरणतोऽवतीर्य । पृथ्व्यां वपुः कनकदण्डमिवाभिपात्य । स्पृष्ट्वा चतुर्मुकुट कोटिभिरङ्घ्रियुग्मं । नत्वा मुदश्रुसुजलैः...
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā tvareṇa nijadhoraṇato pṛthvyāṃ vapuḥ kanakadaṇḍamivābhipātya spṛṣṭvā caturmukuṭa koṭibhiraṅghriyugmaṃ natvā mudaśrusujalaiḥ akṛtābhiṣekam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat ]
[vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat ]
[aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single], [aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]
[aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]
[aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]

translation: As soon as he beheld it, Brahmā hastily alighted from his mount (swan) and lay prostrate before him like a staff of gold on the ground. Touching his pair of feet with the crest of his four crowns, he paid respects to him and bathed his feet with his holy tears of joy.

devanagari: उत्थायोत्थाय कृष्णस्य चिरस्य पादयोः पतन् । आस्ते महित्वं प्राग्दृष्टं स्मृत्वा स्मृत्वा पुनः पुनः ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: utthāyotthāya kṛṣṇasya cirasya pādayoḥ patan āste mahitvaṃ prāgdṛṣṭaṃ smṛtvā smṛtvā punaḥ punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: And repeatedly remembering the great glory of the Lord witnessed by him uptill now, he rose again and again to lay prostrate at his feet for a long time.

devanagari: शनैरथोत्थाय विमृज्य लोचने । मुकुन्दमुद्वीक्ष्य विनम्रकन्धरः । कृताञ्जलिः प्रश्रयवान्समाहितः । सवेपथुर्गद्‍गदयैलतेलया ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: śanairathotthāya vimṛjya locane mukundamudvīkṣya vinamrakandharaḥ kṛtāñjaliḥ praśrayavānsamāhitaḥ savepathurgad‍gadayailatelayā
translation:
Thereupon, rising up gradually, he rubbed his eyes. Looking up intently at Hari but with a bent head, he folded his palms and with great humility and concentration of mind, he, with his body trembling with emotions and in a faltering voice, chanted the praise of the Lord.
[back to top]
